candidate_problems.text: How can 'genuine knowledge' at the system level be operationally distinguished from highly effective recombination? The conjecture relies on emergent explanatory behavior, but it leaves unresolved what non-circular tests would separate real explanation, criticism, and novelty from sophisticated interpolation or memorized pattern reuse.

What specific interaction structures are necessary and sufficient for open criticism to emerge in token-prediction systems? The conjecture points to data, architecture, optimization, prompts, and external criticism, but it remains unclear which feedback loops actually produce corrigibility, cross-domain coherence, and self-error-detection, and which merely simulate them.

Where should epistemic credit be assigned in a coupled human-model system? If knowledge is attributed to the whole training-and-inference system rather than the model alone, an unresolved tension is whether the model itself knows anything, or whether apparent knowledge is parasitic on human-designed objectives, prompts, evaluators, and institutions of criticism.
